hypothesis: If pre-training on large chemical datasets (ChEMBL or ZINC-15) and fine-tuning on a curated Covalent Triazine Framework dataset, coupled with reinforcement learning guided by expert-defined rewards for molecular flatness, symmetry, conjugation, steric hindrance, and pattern matching, then the generated CTF candidates will show higher validity, uniqueness, internal diversity, and novelty, and a higher percentage of candidates passing expert filters (with a lower Fréchet ChemNet Distance) than configurations without such transfer learning and reward shaping, because transfer of broad chemical knowledge plus domain-specific objective guidance biases generation toward chemically plausible, desirable CTF structures.
